t92.subject: Cache config::load() behind mtime gate
t92.description: Replace the uncached read+parse in config::load() (config.rs:636) with a Mutex<Option<Cached>> storing (mtime, parsed Config). On each call, stat the file: if mtime is unchanged from the cached entry, return the clone; otherwise re-read, re-parse, and refresh. This kills the ~50 reads/sec from the tick/render hot path. Keep the public `load() -> Config` signature unchanged. Note: log-level demotion is handled separately in t93 to avoid a same-function edit collision.
t92.acceptance_criteria: 1) `cargo check -p jfc-ui` compiles clean. 2) `cargo test -p jfc-ui --lib config::` passes (existing parser/round-trip tests untouched). 3) New regression test asserts the underlying file read+parse happens exactly once across two back-to-back load() calls with unchanged mtime (via a read counter or cache-hit assertion). 4) Cache invalidates when config.toml mtime changes, preserving the file_watcher hot-reload path. (Live CPU/strace re-profiling moved to the verify task.)
t92.verification_command: cargo check -p jfc-ui && cargo test -p jfc-ui --bin jfc config::

t72.subject: Replace ext/ext2 slop with macro-driven slash-command registry
t72.description: Collapse input/slash_commands{,_ext,_ext2}.rs (3 files, 88 commands, ~2500 lines) into a single macro_rules! registry that is the SOLE source of truth for dispatch + autocomplete metadata. Grounded in the rust-lang Zulip wg-macros thread "Please help me figure out these very old macro guidelines": functions are preferred for handler LOGIC; a macro is the blessed tool when it "generates a static table and a function guaranteed to take action on all items in that list, significantly reducing human error" (kpreid). Macro defined in the SAME module as its use site. Eliminates the drifted second table in render/overlays.rs.
t72.acceptance_criteria: cargo build -p jfc-ui succeeds; cargo test -p jfc-ui passes; ripgrep finds no slash_commands_ext references; render/overlays.rs no longer defines its own SLASH_COMMANDS const

t78.subject: Point autocomplete + /help at the generated SLASH_COMMANDS table
t78.description: Kill the second source of truth. render/overlays.rs:189 currently hand-maintains its own `const SLASH_COMMANDS: &[(&str,&str)]` (~21 of 88 commands, comment literally says "Keep in sync with handlers in input.rs"). Remove it and have slash_matches() / slash_popup() read the macro-generated table from input::slash_commands (re-export as needed). Also update the /help handler (from t75) to ITERATE the generated table instead of a hand-written list, so help can never drift either. This is the core payoff per kpreid: "a macro that generates a static table AND a function guaranteed to take action on all of the items in that list."
t78.acceptance_criteria: render/overlays.rs no longer defines SLASH_COMMANDS; slash_matches reads the generated table; /help iterates the table; autocomplete popup shows all 88 commands; cargo build compiles

Fix by removing the outer `mod tests { ... }` wrapper from the file-based test modules so the content lives at file scope with `use super::*;` directly.

t73.subject: Design the slash_commands! macro + SlashCommand descriptor
t73.description: In a new crates/jfc-ui/src/input/slash_commands/registry.rs (or top of slash_commands.rs), define: (1) a `struct SlashSpec { name: &str, aliases: &[&str], help: &str }` descriptor; (2) a `macro_rules! slash_commands!` that takes rows like `"/clear" [] "clear the conversation" => clear_handler` and emits BOTH (a) a `pub(crate) const SLASH_COMMANDS: &[SlashSpec]` static table for autocomplete/help, AND (b) the `match parts[0] { ... }` dispatch arms calling each async handler. Macro must live in the same module as use site per kpreid's guidance. Handler signature: async fn(app: &mut App, parts: &[&str], text: &str, tx: Option<&mpsc::Sender<AppEvent>>). t85.subject: Fix claim 6: FILE_LOCKS unbounded growth
